page: Microsoft Azure E48a_v4 is an EAv4-series virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor running at 3.35 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes a local storage allocation of 1229 GB, while containing no GPU accelerators. The hypervisor layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, representing a notable tradeoff for single-threaded tasks. However, multi-threaded capabilities are more robust, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores landing in the middle 50%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU encryption tests both register in the top 25%, demonstrating strong performance in parallel processing and cryptographic workloads. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently rank in the middle 50%, alongside average database operations and Redis SET throughput.

Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is structured for memory-intensive workloads. The high memory capacity combined with 1229 GB of local storage makes it suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics. Large-scale LLM inference is a bottleneck, as text generation for medium and large models falls into the bottom 25%. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E48a_v4 offers a dense memory footprint and substantial local storage on a dedicated AMD EPYC platform, making it a viable option for enterprise database hosting and memory-bound parallel processing where peak single-core speed is not the primary requirement.
description: Microsoft Azure E48a_v4 is an EAv4-family virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor operating at 3.35 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with Microsoft Hyper-V, this server includes 1229 GB of local storage and no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance ranks in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and PassMark encryption, rank in the top 25%. Most other metrics, including memory bandwidth, Redis operations, and software compilation, fall within the middle 50%. With its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local storage, the E48a_v4 is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching, and parallel data processing tasks that do not rely heavily on single-threaded execution speed.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48a_v4 is an EAv4 virtual machine with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, offering strong multi-threaded performance for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48a_v4 features 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and substantial local storage for parallel, memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor at 3.35 GHz
384.0 GB of RAM providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
1229 GB of local storage for database and caching workloads
Strong multi-threaded performance, ranking in the top 25% for encryption and decompression
Tradeoff of lower single-core CPU performance, ranking in the bottom 10%
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and parallel data processing
